title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which is due by January 31st. During this period, you should reflect on your achievements, challenges, and areas for improvement over the past year. After self-assessments are submitted, managers will review them and schedule one-on-one meetings to discuss performance feedback from February 1st to February 28th. The final performance reviews will be completed by March 15th. For an effective self-assessment, focus on specific examples of your work, align your achievements with company goals, and be honest about areas needing development. For any questions or assistance, please contact the HR department at hr@company.com.
